summary: High‑energy Dark Star captured on a decent soundboard recording with only a faint hiss.
review: The disc shines thanks to an extraordinary Dark Star that many reviewers call “king‑sized” and “spectacular,” backed by strong performances of Two Souls, Truckin' and BBM.  The source is a soundboard (SBD) with a slight buzz/hiss but overall clear and enjoyable listening.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars, praising the Dark Star and overall playing while noting only minor hiss/buzz in the soundboard source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: A faint buzz/hiss is noted but does not detract from the clarity; overall the mix is crisp and well‑balanced for a partial SBD recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Partial Set II (no Sugar Mag encore) still flows strongly; the centerpiece Dark Star leads into a tight Two Souls and solid Truckin'/BBM sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for driving the Dark Star with stellar guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for “going berzerk” on bass during the Dark Star, described as essential to the performance.
